title: Types of Chemical Reactions
subject: AP Chemistry
objectives: Classify reactions as synthesis, decomposition, single replacement, double replacement, or combustion
Balance chemical equations using inspection and identify coefficients as mole ratios
Recognize oxidation-reduction reactions by tracking electron transfer

sections.heading: Five Major Reaction Types
sections.content:
Chemical reactions rearrange atoms into new combinations. Recognizing the pattern helps you predict products.

Synthesis (combination): Two or more reactants form a single product. A + B в†’ AB. Example: 2Na + Clв‚‚ в†’ 2NaCl. Think of it as building something from parts.

Decomposition: A single compound breaks into two or more simpler substances. AB в†’ A + B. Example: 2Hв‚‚Oв‚‚ в†’ 2Hв‚‚O + Oв‚‚. The reverse of synthesis.

Single replacement: One element replaces another in a compound. A + BC в†’ AC + B. Example: Zn + CuSOв‚„ в†’ ZnSOв‚„ + Cu. Zinc is more reactive than copper, so it displaces copper from solution. The activity series tells you which metals can displace which.

Double replacement (metathesis): Two compounds swap partners. AB + CD в†’ AD + CB. Example: AgNOв‚ѓ + NaCl в†’ AgClв†“ + NaNOв‚ѓ. This typically occurs when a precipitate, gas, or water is formed.

Combustion: A hydrocarbon reacts with Oв‚‚ to produce COв‚‚ and Hв‚‚O. Example: CHв‚„ + 2Oв‚‚ в†’ COв‚‚ + 2Hв‚‚O. Every fossil fuel you burn undergoes combustion.

sections.heading: Balancing a Chemical Equation
sections.content:
Unbalanced: Fe + Oв‚‚ в†’ Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ

Step 1: Count atoms on each side.
  Left: 1 Fe, 2 O    Right: 2 Fe, 3 O

Step 2: Balance Fe by placing a 2 in front of Fe on the left.
  2Fe + Oв‚‚ в†’ Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ   (Fe balanced, O still off: 2 vs 3)

Step 3: Balance O. You need 3 O on the left. With Oв‚‚, try 3/2:
  2Fe + 3/2 Oв‚‚ в†’ Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ

Step 4: Clear fractions by multiplying everything by 2:
  4Fe + 3Oв‚‚ в†’ 2Feв‚‚Oв‚ѓ

Check: Left: 4 Fe, 6 O.  Right: 4 Fe, 6 O. вњ“

The coefficients (4, 3, 2) are mole ratios: 4 moles of iron react with 3 moles of oxygen gas to produce 2 moles of iron(III) oxide.

Balanced equations conserve atoms (Law of Conservation of Mass). Coefficients represent mole ratios, which are the bridge between quantities in stoichiometry problems. Never change subscripts to balance вЂ” only coefficients.

sections.heading: Introduction to Oxidation-Reduction (Redox)
sections.content:
Many reactions involve the transfer of electrons. In oxidation, a substance loses electrons (its oxidation number increases). In reduction, a substance gains electrons (its oxidation number decreases). The mnemonic OIL RIG helps: Oxidation Is Loss, Reduction Is Gain.

In the single replacement reaction Zn + CuВІвЃє в†’ ZnВІвЃє + Cu:
  Zinc loses 2 electrons: Zn в†’ ZnВІвЃє + 2eвЃ» (oxidation)
  Copper gains 2 electrons: CuВІвЃє + 2eвЃ» в†’ Cu (reduction)

The substance that gets oxidized is the reducing agent (it reduces something else by giving away electrons). The substance that gets reduced is the oxidizing agent.

Redox is everywhere: combustion, corrosion (rusting), batteries, photosynthesis, and cellular respiration are all redox processes. Recognizing redox is essential for AP Chemistry because it connects reaction types, electrochemistry, and thermodynamics.

aiContext: AP Chemistry. Use dimensional analysis for all calculations. Stress WHY reactions happen, not just equation balancing. Common confusion: entropy vs enthalpy direction, and applying Le Chatelier to specific perturbations.
